# Preserve per-function Lambda packaging

Lambda handlers retain isolated, per-function archives; deployment speed must not be gained by placing every handler in one service-wide bundle. TypeScript checking moves from deployment-time Webpack plugins into a blocking pull-request QA job, allowing ordinary Node deployment packaging to transpile only. The implementation evaluates a faster esbuild-based path for each eligible service, using every handler as a separate entry point so an archive contains only code reachable from that handler. Anti-virus retains its custom Webpack path because its packaging relies on bespoke conditional and permissions plugins.

"Per service" is a verification and rollback boundary within one implementation task, not a requirement for separate projects or releases. An agent processes all eligible services, records reproducible baseline and candidate package timings, inspects the resulting handler archives, and runs the relevant type checks, tests, and preview validation. A service adopts the faster path only when function identity, required assets, source maps, Sentry integration, dynamic module behaviour, and runtime checks remain valid; otherwise it stays on Webpack as a documented exception without blocking verified services from being migrated.
